title: Turn the joystick button 5 override into a true grab-navigation handoff
summary: 0.6.30 keeps the current CUA focus and trigger-tap behavior intact, but stops treating the joystick button 5 override as a broad navigation unlock. While the override is held, ordinary locomotion stays disabled and VaM's own grab-navigation path is given the handoff instead.
changes: Keep the existing thumbstick-click and joystick button 5 detection paths for the temporary grab-navigation override.
Replace the broad override release with a dedicated grab-navigation handoff state inside the CUA input module.
While the grab-nav override is held, keep ordinary navigation disabled, keep pointers hidden, and avoid running local CUA navigation handling.
Restore the original VaM navigation snapshot cleanly when the grab-nav override ends, then let gaze focus re-engage naturally from the existing rules.
reasoning: The 0.6.29 slice correctly found the operator's button path, but it yielded too broadly and restored normal stick locomotion instead of true grab-navigation.
Official VaM grab-navigation is a separate path from ordinary joystick locomotion, so the correct fix is a narrower handoff state instead of a general navigation unlock.
Keeping the change inside the CUA input module avoids reopening playback, playlists, surfaces, or deployment behavior.
operatorVisibleOutcomes: If the user is looking at the screen and holds the joystick button 5 grab-nav control, ordinary joystick locomotion should stay out of the way while VaM grab-navigation remains available.
When the override ends, gaze focus can re-engage using the existing focus rules instead of forcing an immediate recapture while the override is still active.
knownIssues: This slice does not change playback, slideshow behavior, A-B policy, right-stick volume, or spatial audio.
This slice does not redesign surfaces, panel UI, or TV placement.
